metadata.summary: CLAUDE.md is a project-specific instruction file giving Claude Code context on commands, structure, conventions, and gotchas. A concise CLAUDE.md (under 150 lines) eliminates back-and-forth.

content.title: CLAUDE.md Creation
content.summary: CLAUDE.md is a project-specific instruction file that gives Claude Code the context it needs: commands, structure, conventions, and gotchas. A well-crafted CLAUDE.md (under 150 lines) eliminates the back-and-forth of Claude asking basic questions about the project setup.

why.p: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md at session start. Without it, the agent must infer project structure, commands, and conventions from code — a slow, error-prone process that consumes context budget. A 300+ line CLAUDE.md itself wastes context. The sweet spot: concise, structured, focused on what Claude cannot infer from code alone (commands, env vars, gotchas, naming conventions).

list.item: Starting a new project where Claude Code will be the primary AI assistant.
Inheriting a codebase and needing to orient Claude to existing conventions before making changes.
After a major dependency upgrade or framework migration that invalidates existing instructions.
Setting up per-module CLAUDE.md files in a monorepo where modules have different stacks.
Throwaway scripts or single-file utilities with no ongoing AI-assisted development.
Projects where team policy prohibits AI coding tools entirely.
When an accurate CLAUDE.md already exists — update specific sections rather than recreating.

rule.statement: A CLAUDE.md must include: Commands (bash blocks for dev/test/lint/build), Structure (directory tree, 2 levels), Conventions (naming, imports, types), and Key Files (table of important entry points). Project Overview is optional but useful.
rule.rationale: Commands is the most frequently referenced section during AI-assisted development — agents need it to run tests, start servers, and apply linters. Without it, agents guess at command names and fail silently.

rule.statement: Keep CLAUDE.md under 150 lines total. A file over 300 lines consumes context budget that could be used for code.
rule.rationale: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md at session start. Every line of CLAUDE.md is a line not available for the actual code the agent is working on. Concise routing beats comprehensive documentation.

rule.statement: List every command the agent will need in bash code blocks: dev server, test runner, linter, formatter, build, migration, seed. Group by workflow (Development, Testing, Database, Code Quality).
rule.rationale: Agents need exact commands, not descriptions. "Run the tests" is ambiguous; pytest --cov=src -x is actionable. Include flags that agents typically need (coverage, fail-fast, verbose).

example.title: Commands section format
example.code:
## Commands
```bash # Development make dev # Start development server uvicorn src.main:app --reload --port 8000
# Testing pytest --cov=src --cov-report=html pytest -m "not slow" -x # Fast tests only
# Code Quality ruff check . --fix ruff format . mypy src ```

rule.statement: Show the directory tree to exactly 2 levels. Add one-line comments after important directories. Do not list every file — only directories and their purpose.
rule.rationale: A 3+ level tree in CLAUDE.md wastes lines on detail the agent can discover by reading. Two levels provides enough context for the agent to know where to look without reading a full file listing.

rule.statement: List concrete naming conventions with examples: file case (snake_case.py), class case (PascalCase), function case (snake_case), constants (UPPER_SNAKE_CASE), import order (stdlib, third-party, local).
rule.rationale: Agents follow naming conventions literally. Vague conventions ("use good names") produce inconsistent output. Specific rules with examples produce consistent output that passes linters without manual fixes.

rule.statement: For Python projects: explicitly name active ruff rule groups. Agents need to know T20 means no print() to avoid triggering pre-commit hooks.
rule.rationale: Ruff rule groups have non-obvious names. An agent that adds print() for debugging will fail the T20 check and require a follow-up fix. Documenting the active rules prevents this class of pre-commit failures.

antipattern.description: Too much information: documenting every edge case, every API endpoint, full architecture diagrams in prose.
antipattern.reason: Overwhelming context budget. Put detailed architecture docs in .agents/ and reference them from CLAUDE.md with a single line.

antipattern.description: Stale content: commands that no longer work, directory paths that no longer exist, env vars with wrong names.
antipattern.reason: Stale instructions mislead more than they help. An agent following a stale build command wastes tool calls diagnosing the failure rather than doing productive work.

antipattern.description: Duplicate docs: same information already in README.md reproduced in CLAUDE.md.
antipattern.reason: Creates a maintenance burden and inconsistency. CLAUDE.md should contain only what Claude needs that differs from what a human developer would look up in README.

antipattern.description: No structure: wall of text paragraphs without sections or headings.
antipattern.reason: Agents scan CLAUDE.md for specific sections. Unstructured prose requires parsing the entire file to find a specific command or convention.

antipattern.description: Wrong audience: written for human developers with background explanation and narrative prose.
antipattern.reason: CLAUDE.md is read by an agent, not a human onboarding. Omit explanations of why a technology was chosen; include only what the agent needs to operate correctly.

rule.statement: Root CLAUDE.md in a monorepo must delegate to per-module files using @path/to/AGENTS.md syntax. Never copy module-specific instructions to root.
rule.rationale: Copying module instructions to root creates duplicate maintenance burden and grows the root file past context budget. Each module owns its instructions; root provides routing only.

rule.statement: Review and update CLAUDE.md on every major PR that changes directory layout, build commands, or lint configuration.
rule.rationale: CLAUDE.md is a static snapshot. A 6-month-old file with wrong commands misleads every agent session until it is corrected. Tie updates to the same PR that changes the thing CLAUDE.md describes.

rule.statement: Use <required> notation for environment variables, never example values. Use SECRET_KEY=<required> not SECRET_KEY=abc123.
rule.rationale: Agents may use literal example values found in CLAUDE.md when generating code or configuration. Placeholder notation prevents accidental use of example credentials.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: An agent can auto-generate an initial CLAUDE.md by reading package.json, Makefile, pyproject.toml, .env.example, and directory structure, then filling in the standard skeleton. A follow-up agent validates the output by dry-running each command listed (in a safe environment) and flagging any that error. For monorepos following the faion convention, the agent generates root CLAUDE.md (@AGENTS.md) plus per-module AGENTS.md files with 20-80 line summaries.

Read package.json, Makefile (if present), and the top-level directory structure. Generate a CLAUDE.md for this project using the standard skeleton: Overview (1 paragraph), Tech Stack (table), Commands (bash blocks for dev/test/lint/build), Structure (directory tree, 2 levels), Conventions (naming, imports, types), Key Files (table). Keep total length under 150 lines.

Review CLAUDE.md. For each command listed under Commands, verify it exists in package.json scripts or Makefile. For any command that cannot be verified, mark it [UNVERIFIED]. For any directory listed under Structure that doesn't exist, mark it [MISSING].

list.item: claude (Claude Code): Consumes CLAUDE.md at session start; test with `claude --print "what commands does this project have?"` (https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
tree -L 2: Generate directory tree for Structure section (system package)
jq '.scripts' package.json: Extract npm scripts for Commands section (https://jqlang.github.io/jq/)
make -qp: List all Makefile targets (system package)
python -m poetry show: List Python project dependencies for Tech Stack section (https://python-poetry.org)
Claude Code: OSS CLI, direct consumer; use `claude --print` to test context loading (https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code)
GitHub Copilot Workspace: SaaS (preview), has its own context system; CLAUDE.md not directly consumed
Cursor: SaaS/Electron, uses `.cursorrules`; CLAUDE.md not natively consumed
Write CLAUDE.md for Claude, not for humans: avoid narrative prose, use scannable tables and code blocks instead.
Lead with Commands — it is the most frequently referenced section during AI-assisted development.
Keep the Structure section to 2 levels deep; deeper nesting is noise for an agent that will glob and grep anyway.
Add a "Gotchas" section listing things that have surprised developers (or agents) before: migration order requirements, required but undocumented env vars, test isolation quirks.
Review CLAUDE.md on every major PR that changes directory layout, build commands, or lint configuration.
For Python projects: explicitly name active ruff rule groups in CLAUDE.md — agents need to know T20 = no print() to avoid triggering pre-commit hooks.
For monorepos: root CLAUDE.md delegates to per-module files; never copy module-specific instructions to root.
An agent that generates CLAUDE.md and then immediately commits it bypasses human validation of commands. The generated file should be reviewed before first commit.
If CLAUDE.md references environment variables with example values (SECRET_KEY=abc123), agents may use those literal values in code. Use SECRET_KEY=<required> notation instead.
CLAUDE.md with @AGENTS.md delegation (faion convention) requires Claude Code to support the @ include syntax. Verify version compatibility before deploying this pattern to a new team.
Agents working on a feature branch may have a branch-local CLAUDE.md that differs from main; ensure the file is kept in sync or explicitly scope which branch the instructions apply to.
An outdated CLAUDE.md that references a removed directory causes agents to waste tool calls searching for files that no longer exist.

template.purpose: Minimal CLAUDE.md (~30 lines) for simple single-stack projects.
Standard CLAUDE.md (~100 lines) for FastAPI/Django backends.
Monorepo root CLAUDE.md that delegates to per-app files.
Bash script to dump npm scripts and Makefile targets into CLAUDE.md-ready format.
